#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
OmegA ‚ÄĒ Sovereign Intelligence CLI
R ¬∑ W ¬∑ Ōú ¬∑ Y
"""

import asyncio
import json
import os
import re
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional

import httpx
from prompt_toolkit import PromptSession
from prompt_toolkit.auto_suggest import AutoSuggestFromHistory
from prompt_toolkit.formatted_text import HTML
from prompt_toolkit.history import FileHistory
from prompt_toolkit.key_binding import KeyBindings
from prompt_toolkit.styles import Style
from rich import box
from rich.columns import Columns
from rich.console import Console
from rich.live import Live
from rich.markdown import Markdown
from rich.panel import Panel
from rich.rule import Rule
from rich.syntax import Syntax
from rich.table import Table
from rich.text import Text
from rich.theme import Theme
from rich.tree import Tree

# ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Config ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

ENV_FILES = [
    Path.home() / ".omega" / "one-true.env",
    Path.home() / ".omega" / "gateway.env",
]
READLINE_HISTORY = Path.home() / ".omega" / "cli" / "readline_history"
SESSION_LOG      = Path.home() / ".omega" / "cli" / "history.jsonl"
SESSIONS_DIR     = Path.home() / ".omega" / "sessions"
API_BASE         = os.environ.get("OMEGA_API_URL", "https://omega-sovereign.vercel.app")

for _p in [READLINE_HISTORY.parent, SESSIONS_DIR]:
    _p.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

# ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Risk classification ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

READONLY_CMDS   = {"ls","ll","la","cat","head","tail","echo","pwd","env","printenv",
                   "which","file","find","grep","rg","fd","wc","diff","stat","tree",
                   "git status","git log","git diff","git show","git branch",
                   "ps","top","df","du","free","uname","whoami","id","date","uptime"}
HIGHRISK_PATTERNS = re.compile(
    r"\b(rm|rmdir|mv|cp|chmod|chown|sudo|dd|mkfs|fdisk|parted|"
    r"git\s+push|git\s+reset\s+--hard|git\s+rebase|git\s+force|"
    r"pip\s+install|npm\s+install|apt\s+install|apt-get)\b",
    re.IGNORECASE
)

def classify_risk(cmd: str) -> str:
    base = cmd.strip().split()[0] if cmd.strip() else ""
    if any(cmd.strip().startswith(r) for r in READONLY_CMDS):
        return "readonly"
    if HIGHRISK_PATTERNS.search(cmd):
        return "high"
    return "standard"

# ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Theme ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

THEME = Theme({
    "omega":   "bold #a855f7",
    "user":    "bold #22d4f5",
    "dim":     "dim #4a4947",
    "gold":    "bold #e8af34",
    "green":   "#6bcf7f",
    "red":     "bold #ff6b6b",
    "yellow":  "#ffd93d",
    "muted":   "#797876",
    "border":  "#2a2826",
    "banner":  "bold #22d4f5",
    "high":    "bold #ff6b6b",
    "std":     "#ffd93d",
    "low":     "#6bcf7f",
    "code":    "#22d4f5",
})

console = Console(theme=THEME, highlight=False)

PROMPT_STYLE = Style.from_dict({
    "prompt":       "#22d4f5 bold",
    "prompt.arrow": "#a855f7 bold",
    "":             "#cdccca",
})

# ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Env ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

def load_env():
    for path in ENV_FILES:
        if not path.exists():
            continue
        for line in path.read_text().splitlines():
            line = line.strip()
            if not line or line.startswith("#") or "=" not in line:
                continue
            k, v = line.split("=", 1)
            os.environ.setdefault(k.strip(), v.strip().strip('"').strip("'"))

# ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Session ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

def new_session_id() -> str:
    return f"ő©-{datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d-%H%M')}"

def session_path(sid: str) -> Path:
    return SESSIONS_DIR / f"{sid}.json"

def save_session(sid: str, history: list, state: dict):
    data = {
        "session_id": sid,
        "created_at": state.get("created_at", datetime.now().isoformat()),
        "updated_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
        "messages": history,
        "state": state,
    }
    path = session_path(sid)
    path.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2))

def load_session(sid: str) -> Optional[dict]:
    path = session_path(sid)
    if not path.exists():
        return None
    return json.loads(path.read_text())

def list_sessions() -> list:
    sessions = []
    for p in sorted(SESSIONS_DIR.glob("*.json"), reverse=True)[:20]:
        try:
            d = json.loads(p.read_text())
            sessions.append(d)
        except Exception:
            pass
    return sessions

# ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Git context ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

def git_info() -> str:
    try:
        branch = subprocess.check_output(
            ["git", "rev-parse", "--abbrev-ref", "HEAD"],
            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL, text=True
        ).strip()
        dirty = subprocess.call(
            ["git", "diff", "--quiet"],
            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL
        ) != 0
        return f"{branch}{'*' if dirty else ''}"
    except Exception:
        return ""

last_state: dict = {}

# ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Streaming chat ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

async def stream_response(user: str, history: list, inject_context: str = "") -> str:
    token = os.environ.get("OMEGA_OPERATOR_TOKEN", "")
    headers = {
        "Content-Type": "application/json",
        "x-omega-operator-token": token,
    }
    msg = f"{inject_context}\n\n{user}".strip() if inject_context else user
    payload = {"user": msg, "history": history, "voiceMode": False}

    full_response = ""

    with console.status("[muted]connecting‚Ä¶[/muted]", spinner="dots",
                        spinner_style="#a855f7") as status:
        try:
            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=120) as client:
                async with client.stream(
                    "POST", f"{API_BASE}/api/chat",
                    headers=headers, json=payload,
                ) as resp:
                    # Capture response headers for /state
                    global last_state
                    last_state = {
                        "mode":       resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Mode", "?"),
                        "provider":   resp.headers.get("X-Provider", "?"),
                        "risk":       resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Risk-Score", "?"),
                        "outcome":    resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Outcome", "?"),
                        "session_id": resp.headers.get("X-Session-Id", "?"),
                        "envelope":   resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Envelope-Version", "?"),
                        "phylactery": resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Phylactery-Head", "?"),
                        "stack":      resp.headers.get("X-Omega-Stack", "?"),
                    }

                    if resp.status_code != 200:
                        body = await resp.aread()
                        status.stop()
                        console.print(f"\n[red]  error {resp.status_code}: "
                                      f"{body.decode()[:200]}[/red]\n")
                        return ""

                    status.update("[muted]synthesizing‚Ä¶[/muted]")

                    buf = ""
                    async for chunk in resp.aiter_text():
                        buf += chunk
                        while "\n" in buf:
                            line, buf = buf.split("\n", 1)
                            if line.startswith('data: {"type":"status"'):
                                continue
                            if line.strip():
                                full_response += line + "\n"
                    if buf.strip() and not buf.startswith('data: {"type":"status"'):
                        full_response += buf

        except httpx.ConnectError:
            status.stop()
            console.print(f"\n[red]  connection error ‚ÄĒ {API_BASE} unreachable[/red]\n")
            return ""
        except httpx.TimeoutException:
            status.stop()
            console.print("\n[red]  request timed out[/red]\n")
            return ""
        except Exception as e:
            status.stop()
            console.print(f"\n[red]  error: {e}[/red]\n")
            return ""

    if not full_response.strip():
        return ""

    _render_response(full_response.strip())
    return full_response.strip()


def _render_response(text: str):
    console.print()
    is_markdown = any(m in text for m in ("**", "##", "```", "\n- ", "\n* ", "\n1. ", "> "))
    if is_markdown:
        console.print(Panel(
            Markdown(text),
            border_style="#2a2826",
            padding=(0, 2),
            title="[omega]ő©[/omega]",
            title_align="left",
        ))
    else:
        # Wrap long plain text
        console.print(f"[omega]ő© ‚Äļ[/omega]  {text}")
    console.print()

# ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Shell command execution ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

async def run_shell_command(cmd: str) -> bool:
    """Execute a bang-prefixed command with risk gate. Returns True if executed."""
    risk = classify_risk(cmd)

    if risk == "high":
        console.print(f"\n[high]  ‚ö†  HIGH RISK:[/high] [dim]{cmd}[/dim]")
        console.print("  [muted]This operation is destructive. Continue? [y/N][/muted] ", end="")
        try:
            answer = input().strip().lower()
        except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):
            answer = "n"
        if answer != "y":
            console.print("  [muted]  blocked.[/muted]\n")
            return False
    elif risk == "standard":
        console.print(f"\n[std]  ‚Ėł standard:[/std] [dim]{cmd}[/dim]")
        console.print("  [muted]Execute? [Y/n][/muted] ", end="")
        try:
            answer = input().strip().lower()
        except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):
            answer = "n"
        if answer == "n":
            console.print("  [muted]  skipped.[/muted]\n")
            return False

    console.print()
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, text=True,
                                capture_output=False)
        if result.returncode != 0:
            console.print(f"  [muted]exit code {result.returncode}[/muted]")
    except Exception as e:
        console.print(f"[red]  shell error: {e}[/red]")
    console.print()
    return True

        return

    t = Table(box=box.SIMPLE, border_style="#2a2826",
              title="[omega]saved sessions[/omega]", title_justify="left")

    t.add_column("turns", style="dim", justify="right")
    t.add_column("updated", style="muted")
    t.add_column("model", style="dim")
    for s in sessions:
        turns = len(s.get("messages", [])) // 2
        updated = s.get("updated_at", "")[:16].replace("T", " ")
        model = s.get("state", {}).get("provider", "?")
        t.add_row(s.get("session_id", "?"), str(turns), updated, model)
    console.print(t)
    console.print()

async def cmd_resume(args: str, current_session_id: str, history: list, state: dict):
    sessions = list_sessions()
    if not sessions:
        console.print("\n[muted]  no saved sessions to resume[/muted]\n")
        return current_session_id, history, state

    if args:
        sid = args.strip()
    else:
        # Show picker
        await cmd_sessions()
        console.print("  [muted]enter session id to resume (or press enter to cancel):[/muted] ", end="")
        try:
            sid = input().strip()
        except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):
            sid = ""
        if not sid:
            return current_session_id, history, state

    data = load_session(sid)
    if not data:
        # Try partial match
        for s in sessions:
            if sid in s.get("session_id", ""):
                data = s
                sid = s["session_id"]
                break

    if not data:
        console.print(f"\n[red]  session not found: {sid}[/red]\n")
        return current_session_id, history, state

    loaded_history = data.get("messages", [])
    loaded_state   = data.get("state", {})
    loaded_state["created_at"] = data.get("created_at", datetime.now().isoformat())

    console.print(f"\n[green]  ‚úď resumed:[/green] [dim]{sid}[/dim]  "
                  f"[muted]({len(loaded_history)//2} turns)[/muted]\n")
    # Show last 2 turns
    for msg in loaded_history[-4:]:
        role_tag = "[user]you ‚Äļ[/user]" if msg["role"] == "user" else "[omega]ő© ‚Äļ[/omega]"
        preview = msg["text"][:200].replace("\n", " ")
        console.print(f"  {role_tag}  [dim]{preview}‚Ä¶[/dim]")
    console.print()

    return sid, loaded_history, loaded_state


async def cmd_read(args: str) -> str:
    """Read a file and return its content as context injection."""
    if not args:
        console.print("\n[muted]  usage: /read <path>[/muted]\n")
        return ""
    path = Path(args.strip()).expanduser()
    if not path.exists():
        console.print(f"\n[red]  file not found: {path}[/red]\n")
        return ""
    try:
        content = path.read_text(errors="replace")
        size = len(content)
        if size > 50_000:
            content = content[:50_000] + "\n‚Ä¶ (truncated)"
        # Detect language for syntax highlight
        suffix = path.suffix.lstrip(".")
        lang_map = {"py": "python", "ts": "typescript", "tsx": "tsx",
                    "js": "javascript", "rs": "rust", "go": "go",
                    "json": "json", "md": "markdown", "sh": "bash",
                    "toml": "toml", "yaml": "yaml", "yml": "yaml",
                    "css": "css", "html": "html", "sql": "sql"}
        lang = lang_map.get(suffix, "text")
        console.print()
        console.print(Panel(
            Syntax(content[:3000], lang, theme="monokai",
                   line_numbers=True, word_wrap=True)
            if lang != "text" else Text(content[:3000], style="dim"),
            title=f"[code]{path.name}[/code]",
            border_style="#2a2826",
            subtitle=f"[muted]{size:,} chars ‚ÄĒ injecting into next message[/muted]",
        ))
        console.print()
        return f"[File: {path}]\n```{lang}\n{content}\n```"
    except Exception as e:
        console.print(f"\n[red]  read error: {e}[/red]\n")
        return ""


async def cmd_search(args: str) -> str:
    """Grep the codebase and optionally inject results."""
    if not args:
        console.print("\n[muted]  usage: /search <pattern>[/muted]\n")
        return ""
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["grep", "-rn", "--include=*.py", "--include=*.ts",
             "--include=*.tsx", "--include=*.js", "--include=*.rs",
             "--include=*.go", "--include=*.md",
             "-l", args, "."],
            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10
        )
        files = result.stdout.strip().splitlines()
        if not files:
            console.print(f"\n[muted]  no matches for:[/muted] {args}\n")
            return ""

        # Show matches
        console.print()
        console.print(Panel(
            "\n".join(f"[code]{f}[/code]" for f in files[:30]),
            title=f"[omega]search: {args}[/omega]",
            border_style="#2a2826",
        ))
        console.print()

        # Get actual line matches (first 20)
        result2 = subprocess.run(
            ["grep", "-rn", "--include=*.py", "--include=*.ts",
             "--include=*.tsx", "--include=*.js", "--include=*.rs",
             args, "."],
            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10
        )
        matches = result2.stdout.strip()[:3000]
        return f"[Search results for '{args}']\n{matches}"
    except Exception as e:
        console.print(f"\n[red]  search error: {e}[/red]\n")
        return ""


async def cmd_files(args: str):
    base = Path(args.strip()).expanduser() if args.strip() else Path.cwd()
    if not base.exists():
        console.print(f"\n[red]  path not found: {base}[/red]\n")
        return
    console.print()
    tree = Tree(f"[code]{base.name}/[/code]", guide_style="#2a2826")

    def add_children(node, path: Path, depth: int = 0):
        if depth > 3:
            return
        try:
            items = sorted(path.iterdir(), key=lambda x: (x.is_file(), x.name))
        except PermissionError:
            return
        shown = 0
        for item in items:
            if item.name.startswith(".") or item.name in (
                "node_modules", "__pycache__", ".git", "dist", ".next",
                "venv", ".venv", "target", "build"
            ):
                continue
            if shown >= 20:
                node.add("[muted]‚Ä¶[/muted]")
                break
            if item.is_dir():
                branch = node.add(f"[gold]{item.name}/[/gold]")
                add_children(branch, item, depth + 1)
            else:
                size = item.stat().st_size
                size_str = f"[muted]{size:,}b[/muted]" if size < 10000 else f"[muted]{size//1024}kb[/muted]"
                node.add(f"[dim]{item.name}[/dim]  {size_str}")
            shown += 1

    add_children(tree, base)
    console.print(Panel(tree, border_style="#2a2826",
                        title=f"[code]{base}[/code]", title_align="left"))
    console.print()


async def cmd_dashboard(session_id: str, history: list):
    console.print()
    token = os.environ.get("OMEGA_OPERATOR_TOKEN", "")
    mode = "operator" if token else "public"
    git  = git_info()

    # System info
    try:
        import psutil
        cpu  = psutil.cpu_percent(interval=0.1)
        ram  = psutil.virtual_memory()
        ram_str = f"{ram.used // 1024**2:,}MB / {ram.total // 1024**2:,}MB ({ram.percent:.0f}%)"
        cpu_str = f"{cpu:.0f}%"
    except ImportError:
        cpu_str = "n/a"
        ram_str = "n/a"

    info = Table(box=None, padding=(0, 2), show_header=False)
    info.add_column(style="muted", no_wrap=True)
    info.add_column(style="dim")
    info.add_row("session",  session_id)
    info.add_row("mode",     mode)
    info.add_row("endpoint", API_BASE)
    info.add_row("git",      git or "none")
    info.add_row("cwd",      str(Path.cwd()))
    info.add_row("turns",    str(len(history) // 2))
    info.add_row("cpu",      cpu_str)
    info.add_row("ram",      ram_str)
    if last_state:
        info.add_row("provider", last_state.get("provider", "?"))
        info.add_row("risk",     last_state.get("risk", "?"))
        info.add_row("outcome",  last_state.get("outcome", "?"))

    console.print(Panel(info, title="[omega]ő© dashboard[/omega]",
                        border_style="#2a2826", title_align="left"))
    console.print()


async def cmd_history_show(history: list):
    if not history:
        console.print("\n[muted]  no history yet[/muted]\n")
        return
    console.print()
    for i in range(0, len(history), 2):
        user_msg  = history[i]["text"] if i < len(history) else ""
        omega_msg = history[i+1]["text"] if i+1 < len(history) else ""
        console.print(f"  [dim]{i//2 + 1}.[/dim] [user]you ‚Äļ[/user] "
                      f"[dim]{user_msg[:100]}[/dim]")
        if omega_msg:
            console.print(f"     [omega]ő© ‚Äļ[/omega] "
                          f"[dim]{omega_msg[:100].replace(chr(10),' ')}‚Ä¶[/dim]")
        console.print()

# ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Key bindings ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

def make_bindings() -> KeyBindings:
    kb = KeyBindings()

    @kb.add("c-l")
    def _clear(event):
        event.app.renderer.reset()
        os.system("clear")

    return kb

# ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ REPL ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

async def repl(resume_id: Optional[str] = None):
    session: PromptSession = PromptSession(
        history=FileHistory(str(READLINE_HISTORY)),
        auto_suggest=AutoSuggestFromHistory(),
        style=PROMPT_STYLE,
        key_bindings=make_bindings(),
        enable_history_search=True,
        mouse_support=False,
    )

    session_id = resume_id or new_session_id()
    history: list = []
    state: dict   = {"created_at": datetime.now().isoformat()}

    # Resume if requested
    if resume_id:
        data = load_session(resume_id)
        if data:
            history = data.get("messages", [])
            state   = data.get("state", state)
            state["created_at"] = data.get("created_at", state["created_at"])

    pending_context = ""   # injected by /read or /search
    save_counter    = 0

    print_banner(session_id)

    while True:
        try:
            line = await session.prompt_async(
                HTML("<prompt>you</prompt> <prompt.arrow>‚Äļ</prompt.arrow> ")
            )
        except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):
            save_session(session_id, history, state)
            console.print(f"\n[muted]  session saved ‚Üí {session_id}[/muted]\n")
            return

        line = line.strip()
        if not line:
            continue

        # ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Bang commands ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
        if line.startswith("!"):
            await run_shell_command(line[1:].strip())
            continue

        # ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Slash commands ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
        parts = line.split(None, 1)
        cmd   = parts[0].lower()
        args  = parts[1] if len(parts) > 1 else ""

        if cmd in ("/exit", "/quit", "exit", "quit"):
            save_session(session_id, history, state)
            console.print(f"\n[muted]  session saved ‚Üí {session_id}[/muted]\n")
            return

        if cmd == "/new":
            save_session(session_id, history, state)
            history.clear()
            pending_context = ""
            session_id = new_session_id()
            state = {"created_at": datetime.now().isoformat()}
            console.print(f"\n[muted]  new session:[/muted] [dim]{session_id}[/dim]\n")
            continue

        if cmd == "/help":
            await cmd_help()
            continue

        if cmd == "/state":
            await cmd_state()
            continue

        if cmd == "/chain":
            await cmd_chain()
            continue

        if cmd == "/sessions":
            await cmd_sessions()
            continue

        if cmd == "/resume":
            session_id, history, state = await cmd_resume(
                args, session_id, history, state
            )
            continue

        if cmd == "/save":
            save_session(session_id, history, state)
            console.print(f"\n[green]  ‚úď saved:[/green] [dim]{session_id}[/dim]\n")
            continue

        if cmd == "/read":
            pending_context = await cmd_read(args)
            if pending_context:
                console.print(
                    "  [muted]file loaded ‚ÄĒ it will be injected into your next message[/muted]\n"
                )
            continue

        if cmd == "/search":
            pending_context = await cmd_search(args)
            if pending_context:
                console.print(
                    "  [muted]results loaded ‚ÄĒ injecting into your next message[/muted]\n"
                )
            continue

        if cmd == "/files":
            await cmd_files(args)
            continue

        if cmd == "/dashboard":
            await cmd_dashboard(session_id, history)
            continue

        if cmd in ("/history", "/log"):
            await cmd_history_show(history)
            continue

        # ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Unknown slash command ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
        if cmd.startswith("/"):
            console.print(f"\n[muted]  unknown command: {cmd}  (try /help)[/muted]\n")
            continue

        # ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Send to OmegA ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
        inject  = pending_context
        pending_context = ""

        reply = await stream_response(line, history, inject_context=inject)

        if reply:
            history.append({"role": "user",  "text": line})
            history.append({"role": "omega", "text": reply})
            state["provider"] = last_state.get("provider", "?")

            # Auto-save every 10 turns
            save_counter += 1
            if save_counter % 10 == 0:
                save_session(session_id, history, state)

# ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ One-shot ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

async def one_shot(prompt: str) -> int:
    reply = await stream_response(prompt, [])
    return 0 if reply else 1

# ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Entry ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

def main():
    load_env()
    args = sys.argv[1:]

    if not args:
        if not sys.stdin.isatty():
            prompt = sys.stdin.read().strip()
            if prompt:
                return asyncio.run(one_shot(prompt))
        asyncio.run(repl())
        return 0

    if args[0] in ("-h", "--help", "help"):
        console.print("[user]omega[/user]                    interactive REPL")
        console.print('[user]omega "question"[/user]         one-shot query')
        console.print("[user]omega resume[/user]             pick a session to resume")
        console.print("[user]omega resume ő©-2026-...[/user]  resume specific session")
        return 0

    if args[0] == "resume":
        sid = args[1] if len(args) > 1 else None
        asyncio.run(repl(resume_id=sid))
        return 0

    return asyncio.run(one_shot(" ".join(args)))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    try:
        sys.exit(main())
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        console.print("\n[muted]  goodbye.[/muted]\n")
        sys.exit(0)

input.content:
# OmegA CLI Enhancement Prompt (Part 1/4)

**Copy and paste this into Claude Code CLI to upgrade the OmegA operator terminal.**

---

You are OmegA's command-line interface (CLI) designer and implementer. The current CLI is functional but needs comprehensive upgrades for better UX, agentic capability, and operator experience.

## Current State
- Location: `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/bin/omega`
- Language: Python 3 with Rich, httpx, prompt_toolkit
- Features: Chat interface, session persistence, git context, risk classification
- Issues: Error 500 on large prompts, static banner, limited command introspection

## Phase 1: Core UX Enhancements

### 1.1 Dynamic Command Suggestions
**Goal**: Show smart command suggestions based on current context (cwd, recent commands, available skills).

**Implementation**:
- Read user's recent command history
- Query OmegA's 165-skill manifest to suggest relevant skills
- Use `prompt_toolkit.auto_suggest.AutoSuggestFromHistory` with custom filtering
- Show suggestions inline with context tags: `[skill]`, `[command]`, `[history]`

**Example**:
```
you ‚Äļ npm build
                    ‚Üď suggestions (based on last error):
                    [skill] npm-smoke-test-fixer
                    [command] npm install --legacy-peer-deps
                    [history] npm test
you ‚Äļ
```

### 1.2 Smart Error Context
**Goal**: When errors occur (500, connection, timeout), provide actionable recovery steps.

**Implementation**:
- For error 500: detect payload size, suggest chunking or file-based approach
- For connection errors: show API_BASE, retry logic, fallback to offline mode
- For timeout: estimate payload processing time, offer async option
- Add a `/debug` command to show request/response details

**Example**:
```
error 500: internal server error
hint: request size ~150KB (exceeds safe limit of 100KB)
‚Üí Try: split into 2-3 smaller prompts
‚Üí Or: save prompt to file and use /file <path> command
retry? (y/n):
```

### 1.3 Enhanced Session Awareness
**Goal**: Show session state context at prompt, track tokens/cost, offer session export.

**Implementation**:
- Display session metadata in status line: `[tokens: 2.5K / cost: $0.003] [db: 26.2K entries]`
- Add `/session export` to save session as markdown or JSON
- Add `/session stats` to show token usage, response times, model used
- Persist session state to `~/.omega/sessions/<sid>.json` with full history

### 1.4 Rich Response Rendering
**Goal**: Render different response types (markdown, code, JSON, tables) with syntax highlighting and live updates.

**Implementation**:
- Detect response content type (markdown, yaml, code blocks, tables)
- Use `rich.syntax.Syntax` for code blocks with language detection
- Use `rich.table.Table` for structured data
- Add live progress rendering for long-running operations (ingest, db checks)

---

## Phase 2: Agentic Capabilities

### 2.1 Skill Invocation from CLI
**Goal**: Run any of the 165 skills directly from the CLI with parameter passing.

**Implementation**:
- Add `/skill <name> [args]` command
- Parse skill manifest at startup
- Route to local agent OS for execution
- Stream skill output back to CLI with progress indicators

**Example**:
```
you ‚Äļ /skill resume_sweep2 --monitor --db2-threshold 85

[SKILL] Invoking resume_sweep2 with args: --monitor --db2-threshold 85
‚Ü≥ [resume_sweep2] OMEGA INBOX INGEST RESUMPTION
  ‚úď Environment checks passed
  ‚úď DB2 utilization: 47.3%
  [‚Ėď‚Ėď‚Ėď‚ĖĎ‚ĖĎ‚ĖĎ‚ĖĎ‚ĖĎ‚ĖĎ] 30% of INBOX processed (890/2963 items)
  ...
```

### 2.2 Agent Delegation Interface
**Goal**: Delegate complex tasks to other agents (, Codex, Aider) with context preservation.

**Implementation**:
- Add `/delegate <agent> <task>` command
- Pass current session context, git state, and task to agent
- Display agent's work in real-time (streaming output)
- Save delegation results to session

**Example**:
```
you ‚Äļ /delegate codex "audit OmegA-Architecture README against code"

[DELEGATE] Sending to Codex...
[Codex] Scanning OmegA-Architecture for spec/code drift...
  ‚úď Verified: "Uses Neon Postgres" (found in package.json)
  ‚úó Missing: "Implements rate limiting" (no middleware found)
  ‚úď Verified: "Rust gateway layer" (found in gateway/)
[Codex] Audit complete. See full report in /tmp/audit-2026-03-30.md
```

### 2.3 Workflow Chaining
**Goal**: Chain skills and commands together with dependency management.

**Implementation**:
- Add `/chain [skill1] ‚Üí [skill2] ‚Üí [skill3]` syntax
- Track outputs from each step
- Offer variable substitution: `{prev.output}`, `{session.db}`, etc.
- Validate chain DAG before execution

**Example**:
```
you ‚Äļ /chain repo-cartographer ‚Üí security-threat-model ‚Üí spec-to-code-auditor

[CHAIN] 3-step workflow detected
  Step 1: repo-cartographer [5 min]
  Step 2: security-threat-model [3 min]
  Step 3: spec-to-code-auditor [2 min]
  Est. total: 10 min
execute? (y/n): y
```

### 2.4 Persistent Memory Integration
**Goal**: CLI reads from and writes to OmegA's memory database, making it context-aware.

**Implementation**:
- Connect to `OMEGA_DB2_URL` at startup
- On each prompt, query for related memories (embedding similarity)
- Show relevant memories as context: `[recall] Last npm fix: use --legacy-peer-deps`
- Save CLI interactions to memory for future context

**Example**:
```
you ‚Äļ npm build

# OmegA CLI Enhancement Prompt (Part 1/4)

**Copy and paste this into Claude Code CLI to upgrade the OmegA operator terminal.**

---

You are OmegA's command-line interface (CLI) designer and implementer. The current CLI is functional but needs comprehensive upgrades for better UX, agentic capability, and operator experience.

## Current State
- Location: `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/bin/omega`
- Language: Python 3 with Rich, httpx, prompt_toolkit
- Features: Chat interface, session persistence, git context, risk classification
- Issues: Error 500 on large prompts, static banner, limited command introspection

## Phase 1: Core UX Enhancements

### 1.1 Dynamic Command Suggestions
**Goal**: Show smart command suggestions based on current context (cwd, recent commands, available skills).

**Implementation**:
- Read user's recent command history
- Query OmegA's 165-skill manifest to suggest relevant skills
- Use `prompt_toolkit.auto_suggest.AutoSuggestFromHistory` with custom filtering
- Show suggestions inline with context tags: `[skill]`, `[command]`, `[history]`

**Example**:
```
you ‚Äļ npm build
                    ‚Üď suggestions (based on last error):
                    [skill] npm-smoke-test-fixer
                    [command] npm install --legacy-peer-deps
                    [history] npm test
you ‚Äļ
```

### 1.2 Smart Error Context
**Goal**: When errors occur (500, connection, timeout), provide actionable recovery steps.

**Implementation**:
- For error 500: detect payload size, suggest chunking or file-based approach
- For connection errors: show API_BASE, retry logic, fallback to offline mode
- For timeout: estimate payload processing time, offer async option
- Add a `/debug` command to show request/response details

**Example**:
```
error 500: internal server error
hint: request size ~150KB (exceeds safe limit of 100KB)
‚Üí Try: split into 2-3 smaller prompts
‚Üí Or: save prompt to file and use /file <path> command
retry? (y/n):
```

### 1.3 Enhanced Session Awareness
**Goal**: Show session state context at prompt, track tokens/cost, offer session export.

**Implementation**:
- Display session metadata in status line: `[tokens: 2.5K / cost: $0.003] [db: 26.2K entries]`
- Add `/session export` to save session as markdown or JSON
- Add `/session stats` to show token usage, response times, model used
- Persist session state to `~/.omega/sessions/<sid>.json` with full history

### 1.4 Rich Response Rendering
**Goal**: Render different response types (markdown, code, JSON, tables) with syntax highlighting and live updates.

**Implementation**:
- Detect response content type (markdown, yaml, code blocks, tables)
- Use `rich.syntax.Syntax` for code blocks with language detection
- Use `rich.table.Table` for structured data
- Add live progress rendering for long-running operations (ingest, db checks)

---

## Phase 2: Agentic Capabilities

### 2.1 Skill Invocation from CLI
**Goal**: Run any of the 165 skills directly from the CLI with parameter passing.

**Implementation**:
- Add `/skill <name> [args]` command
- Parse skill manifest at startup
- Route to local agent OS for execution
- Stream skill output back to CLI with progress indicators

**Example**:
```
you ‚Äļ /skill resume_sweep2 --monitor --db2-threshold 85

[SKILL] Invoking resume_sweep2 with args: --monitor --db2-threshold 85
‚Ü≥ [resume_sweep2] OMEGA INBOX INGEST RESUMPTION
  ‚úď Environment checks passed
  ‚úď DB2 utilization: 47.3%
  [‚Ėď‚Ėď‚Ėď‚ĖĎ‚ĖĎ‚ĖĎ‚ĖĎ‚ĖĎ‚ĖĎ] 30% of INBOX processed (890/2963 items)
  ...
```

### 2.2 Agent Delegation Interface
**Goal**: Delegate complex tasks to other agents (, Codex, Aider) with context preservation.

**Implementation**:
- Add `/delegate <agent> <task>` command
- Pass current session context, git state, and task to agent
- Display agent's work in real-time (streaming output)
- Save delegation results to session

**Example**:
```
you ‚Äļ /delegate codex "audit OmegA-Architecture README against code"

[DELEGATE] Sending to Codex...
[Codex] Scanning OmegA-Architecture for spec/code drift...
  ‚úď Verified: "Uses Neon Postgres" (found in package.json)
  ‚úó Missing: "Implements rate limiting" (no middleware found)
  ‚úď Verified: "Rust gateway layer" (found in gateway/)
[Codex] Audit complete. See full report in /tmp/audit-2026-03-30.md
```

### 2.3 Workflow Chaining
**Goal**: Chain skills and commands together with dependency management.

**Implementation**:
- Add `/chain [skill1] ‚Üí [skill2] ‚Üí [skill3]` syntax
- Track outputs from each step
- Offer variable substitution: `{prev.output}`, `{session.db}`, etc.
- Validate chain DAG before execution

**Example**:
```
you ‚Äļ /chain repo-cartographer ‚Üí security-threat-model ‚Üí spec-to-code-auditor

[CHAIN] 3-step workflow detected
  Step 1: repo-cartographer [5 min]
  Step 2: security-threat-model [3 min]
  Step 3: spec-to-code-auditor [2 min]
  Est. total: 10 min
execute? (y/n): y
```

### 2.4 Persistent Memory Integration
**Goal**: CLI reads from and writes to OmegA's memory database, making it context-aware.

**Implementation**:
- Connect to `OMEGA_DB2_URL` at startup
- On each prompt, query for related memories (embedding similarity)
- Show relevant memories as context: `[recall] Last npm fix: use --legacy-peer-deps`
- Save CLI interactions to memory for future context

**Example**:
```
you ‚Äļ npm build

[recall] Previous npm errors solved with: --legacy-peer-deps flag
[recall] DB status: 47.3% utilization (healthy)
[skill hint] Related: npm-smoke-test-fixer

ő© ‚Äļ
```

---

**Part 2 coming next: Advanced features, architecture, and deployment instructions.**

Save this file and paste when ready. Do NOT paste all 4 parts at once (will trigger 500 error).

# OmegA CLI Enhancement Prompt (Part 2/4)

## Phase 3: Advanced Features

### 3.1 Real-Time DB & System Monitoring
**Goal**: Built-in dashboard for DB status, ingest progress, API health.

**Implementation**:
- Add `/monitor` command that shows live metrics in a panel:
  ```
  ‚ĒĆ‚ĒÄ OmegA Status Dashboard ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚Ēź
  ‚Ēā DB1 (omega-personal):    ‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚ĖĎ‚ĖĎ 87.3% (446 MB / 512 MB)  ‚Ēā
  ‚Ēā DB2 (omega-technical):   ‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚ĖĎ‚ĖĎ‚ĖĎ‚ĖĎ‚ĖĎ‚ĖĎ 47.3% (242 MB / 512 MB)   ‚Ēā
  ‚Ēā Memory entries:          26,143 (avg importance: 0.82)        ‚Ēā
  ‚Ēā API health:              ‚úď OK (latency: 142ms)                ‚Ēā
  ‚Ēā Session age:             3h 24m (tokens: 12.5K / $0.047)      ‚Ēā
  ‚Ēā                                                                ‚Ēā
  ‚Ēā [‚ÜĎ Ingest] [Expand DB] [Export] [Settings]                   ‚Ēā
  ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚Ēė
  ```
- Use `rich.live.Live` for real-time updates
- Add `/monitor --interval 5` for custom refresh rate
- Add `/monitor --export-csv` to save metrics

### 3.2 File-Based Prompt Management
**Goal**: Handle large prompts by saving to file and referencing instead of embedding in JSON.

**Implementation**:
- Add `/prompt-file <path>` to load prompt from file
- Add `/prompt-save <name>` to save current prompt history to `~/.omega/prompts/<name>.md`
- Add `/prompt-template <name>` to use pre-built templates (e.g., "audit-review", "feature-spec")
- Show file size warning: `[WARN] Prompt size 245KB ‚ÄĒ recommend chunking`

**Example**:
```
you ‚Äļ /prompt-file /tmp/comprehensive_upgrade.md

[LOAD] Reading /tmp/comprehensive_upgrade.md (187 KB)
[WARN] Large file detected. Chunking into 3 segments...
  Segment 1: 62 KB (phases 1-2)
  Segment 2: 65 KB (phases 3-4)
  Segment 3: 60 KB (implementation + appendix)
Ready to send? (y/n):
```

### 3.3 Git Integration & Diff Preview
**Goal**: Show code diffs, branch context, and git operations from CLI.

**Implementation**:
- Add `/git status` shorthand for repo status
- Add `/git-diff [file]` to show and paginate diffs
- Add `/git-commit [msg]` to stage and commit from CLI
- Add `/push-pr [title]` to create PR with auto-generated description

**Example**:
```
you ‚Äļ /git-diff omega/cli/omega

[DIFF] Showing changes in OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/bin/omega

@@@ -164,10 +164,12 @@@ def print_banner(session_id: str):
   def print_banner(session_id: str):
-      console.print("  [banner]‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚Ėą‚ēó[/banner]")
+      colors = ["#22d4f5", "#a855f7", "#ec4899", ...]
+      for i, line in enumerate(banner_lines):
+          color = colors[i % len(colors)]
+          console.print(f"  [{color}]{line}[/{color}]")

commit? (y/n):
```

### 3.4 Multi-Modal Input & Output
**Goal**: Support voice commands, image uploads for vision analysis, and raw code/data pasting.

**Implementation**:
- Add `/voice` mode for speech-to-text (requires microphone)
- Add `/image <path>` to upload image for vision analysis: "Describe this screenshot"
- Add `/paste` to enter multi-line mode (Ctrl+D to end)
- Add `/code <language>` to start syntax-highlighted code block input

**Example**:
```
you ‚Äļ /code python

[PASTE] Python mode. Type code, end with Ctrl+D:

def resume_ingest():
    db = connect(OMEGA_DB2_URL)
    sweep = db.query("...")
    for item in sweep:
        process(item)
^D

[CODE] Received 156 bytes of Python code. Analyze?
```

### 3.5 Response Streaming & Pagination
**Goal**: Stream long responses, allow pausing and resuming, rich formatting.

**Implementation**:
- Real-time streaming with pause/resume: `[pause]`, `[space] to continue`, `[q] to quit`
- Auto-paginate long responses into sections
- Syntax highlighting for code blocks with line numbers
- Table rendering with sorting: `/sort column [asc|desc]`

---

## Phase 4: Architecture & Integration

### 4.1 Event-Driven Architecture
**Goal**: CLI becomes event sink for external triggers (webhooks, scheduled tasks).

**Implementation**:
- Internal event bus: `on("ingest.progress")`, `on("db.alert")`, `on("api.error")`
- Register handlers: `/on ingest.complete ‚Üí /delegate  "summarize ingest results"`
- Webhook receiver on localhost:8765 for external triggers
- Emit telemetry to ERGON.md for multi-agent awareness

### 4.2 Configuration & Theming
**Goal**: Customizable colors, key bindings, aliases, and environment variables.

**Implementation**:
- `~/.omega/cli/config.toml` for CLI settings
- `~/.omega/cli/theme.json` for color customization
- `~/.omega/cli/aliases.txt` for command shortcuts
- Built-in editor: `/config edit` opens config in $EDITOR

**Example config**:
```toml
[interface]
banner_colorshift = true
theme = "dark"
pagination_size = 50

[api]
timeout_seconds = 120
max_payload_kb = 100
retry_count = 3

[skills]
auto_suggest = true
manifest_refresh = "startup"

[monitoring]
enable_metrics = true
db_check_interval = 10
alert_threshold = 85
```

### 4.3 Plugin System
**Goal**: Allow operators to write custom CLI commands as Python plugins.

**Implementation**:
- Plugin directory: `~/.omega/cli/plugins/`
- Plugin template: `class OmegaPlugin: def register(cli) ‚Üí cli.add_command(...)`
- Hot-reload: `/plugin reload <name>`
- Plugin store: `/plugin install <github-url>`

**Example plugin**:
```python
class MyPlugin(OmegaPlugin):
    def register(cli):
        @cli.command("/mycommand")
        def my_command(args):
            print("Custom plugin command!")
```

### 4.4 Audit & Compliance Logging
**Goal**: Every CLI action is logged to ERGON.md with checksums for integrity.

**Implementation**:
- Append to `/home/mega/NEXUS/ERGON.md` after each major command
- Format: `[timestamp] [CLI] <action> (exit_code: 0, duration: 2.5s, hash: abc123...)`
- Include: command, user, exit code, duration, any side effects
- Enable compliance review: `/audit --last 24h` shows all recent actions

**Example log entry**:
```
[2026-03-30 14:32] [CLI] resume_sweep2 (exit_code: 0, duration: 847s)
  ‚Üí Items processed: 2,143
  ‚Üí DB2 utilization: 47.3% (was 28.1%)
  ‚Üí Peak memory: 512 MB
  hash: 7f4a9c2e1b8d6f3a
```

---

## Phase 5: Deployment & Documentation

### 5.1 Version Management
**Goal**: Track CLI version, auto-update, migration guides for breaking changes.

**Implementation**:
- Version file: `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/bin/VERSION` (e.g., `2.1.0`)
- Semver compliance and changelog at `CODE/CHANGELOG.md`
- `/upgrade` checks for new version and pulls latest code
- Migration guide auto-display when major version changes

### 5.2 Testing & Validation
**Goal**: CLI has comprehensive tests, validation hooks, and smoke tests.

**Implementation**:
- Unit tests: test all commands with mock API responses
- Integration tests: test real API calls against staging
- Smoke tests: `/test smoke` runs quick validation
- Coverage tracking: `/test coverage`

---

**Part 3 coming next: Detailed implementation roadmap and timeline.**

Save and digest this part before moving to Part 3. These two parts cover the full feature spec.

## Implementation Roadmap

### Sprint 1 (Week 1): Core UX Fixes
**Effort**: 16 hours | **Blocker**: None

# OmegA CLI Enhancement Prompt (Part 4/4 ‚ÄĒ Final)

## Quick Implementation Snippets

### Snippet 1: Smart Command Suggestions
```python
# In omega_cli/commands.py

def get_smart_suggestions(user_input: str, history: list) -> list:
    """
    Return 3‚Äď5 contextual command suggestions based on:
    1. Recent command history
    2. Skill manifest matches
    3. Current git state
    4. Error recovery patterns
    """
    suggestions = []

    # Load skill manifest
    manifest_path = Path.home() / "OMEGA_WORKSPACE" / "claude-skill-bundle" / "skills" / "skills-manifest.json"
    try:
        manifest = json.loads(manifest_path.read_text())
        skills = manifest.get("skills", {})
    except:
        skills = {}

    # Fuzzy match input against skill names
    from difflib import get_close_matches
    matches = get_close_matches(user_input.lower(), list(skills.keys()), n=2, cutoff=0.6)
    for match in matches:
        suggestions.append({
            "type": "skill",
            "label": f"/skill {match}",
            "description": skills[match].get("description", ""),
        })

    # Suggest based on recent error patterns
    if history and "error" in history[-1].get("content", "").lower():
        error_msg = history[-1]["content"]
        if "npm" in error_msg or "build" in error_msg:
            suggestions.append({
                "type": "skill",
                "label": "/skill npm-smoke-test-fixer",
                "description": "Diagnose and fix npm build failures",
            })
        if "db" in error_msg or "database" in error_msg:
            suggestions.append({
                "type": "monitor",
                "label": "/monitor",
                "description": "Check database status",
            })

    return suggestions[:5]  # Return top 5

# In main prompt loop:
suggestions = get_smart_suggestions(user_input, history)
if suggestions:
    console.print("[dim]Suggestions:[/dim]")
    for i, s in enumerate(suggestions, 1):
        console.print(f"  {i}. [{s['type']}] {s['label']} ‚ÄĒ {s['description']}")
```

### Snippet 2: Real-Time DB Monitoring
```python
# In omega_cli/monitoring.py

import psycopg2
from rich.live import Live
from rich.table import Table

def monitor_databases(interval: int = 10) -> None:
    """
    Real-time dashboard showing DB1/DB2 utilization, API health, session metrics.
    """
    db1_url = os.environ.get("OMEGA_DB_URL")
    db2_url = os.environ.get("OMEGA_DB2_URL")

    def get_table():
        table = Table(title="OmegA Status Dashboard")
        table.add_column("Component", style="cyan")
        table.add_column("Status", style="magenta")
        table.add_column("Metrics", style="green")

        # DB1 status
        try:
            conn1 = psycopg2.connect(db1_url)
            cur1 = conn1.cursor()
            cur1.execute("SELECT pg_database_size(current_database()) / (512.0 * 1024 * 1024) * 100")
            db1_util = cur1.fetchone()[0]
            conn1.close()
            status1 = "ūüüĘ OK" if db1_util < 90 else "ūüĒī CRITICAL"
            table.add_row("DB1 (personal)", status1, f"{db1_util:.1f}%")
        except:
            table.add_row("DB1 (personal)", "‚ö†ÔłŹ ERROR", "Connection failed")

        # DB2 status
        try:
            conn2 = psycopg2.connect(db2_url)
            cur2 = conn2.cursor()
            cur2.execute("SELECT pg_database_size(current_database()) / (512.0 * 1024 * 1024) * 100")
            db2_util = cur2.fetchone()[0]
            conn2.close()
            status2 = "ūüüĘ OK" if db2_util < 85 else ("ūüü° HIGH" if db2_util < 90 else "ūüĒī CRITICAL")
            table.add_row("DB2 (technical)", status2, f"{db2_util:.1f}%")
        except:
            table.add_row("DB2 (technical)", "‚ö†ÔłŹ ERROR", "Connection failed")

        # API health
        try:
            resp = httpx.get(f"{API_BASE}/health", timeout=5)
            api_status = "ūüüĘ OK" if resp.status_code == 200 else "ūüü° DEGRADED"
            latency = f"{resp.elapsed.total_seconds() * 1000:.0f}ms"
        except:
            api_status = "ūüĒī DOWN"
            latency = "‚ÄĒ"
        table.add_row("API Gateway", api_status, f"latency: {latency}")

        return table

    with Live(get_table(), refresh_per_second=0.2, console=console) as live:
        try:
            while True:
                time.sleep(interval)
                live.update(get_table())
        except KeyboardInterrupt:
            console.print("\n[green]‚úď Monitoring stopped[/green]")
```

### Snippet 3: Skill Invocation
```python
# In omega_cli/skills.py

async def invoke_skill(skill_name: str, args: list) -> str:
    """
    Route a skill invocation to the local Agent OS.
    Returns the skill's output.
    """
    # Validate skill exists in manifest
    manifest_path = Path.home() / "OMEGA_WORKSPACE" / "claude-skill-bundle" / "skills" / "skills-manifest.json"
    manifest = json.loads(manifest_path.read_text())
    if skill_name not in manifest.get("skills", {}):
        return f"ERROR: Skill '{skill_name}' not found in manifest"

    # Call Agent OS via /skill route
    payload = {
        "skill": skill_name,
        "args": args,
        "session_id": current_session_id,
    }

    try:
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=300) as client:
            async with client.stream(
                "POST", f"{API_BASE}/skills/{skill_name}",
                json=payload,
            ) as resp:
                output = ""
                async for chunk in resp.aiter_text():
                    output += chunk
                    console.print(chunk, end="")
                return output
    except Exception as e:
        return f"ERROR: {str(e)}"
```

### Snippet 4: ERGON.md Audit Logging
```python
# In omega_cli/audit.py

import hashlib

def log_to_ergon(action: str, exit_code: int, duration_sec: float, details: dict = None) -> None:
    """
    Append an audit entry to ~/NEXUS/ERGON.md with checksum for integrity.
    """
    ergon_path = Path.home() / "NEXUS" / "ERGON.md"

    # Build log entry
    timestamp = datetime.now().isoformat()
    entry_data = {
        "timestamp": timestamp,
        "cli": True,
        "action": action,
        "user": os.getenv("USER", "unknown"),
        "exit_code": exit_code,
        "duration_sec": duration_sec,
        "details": details or {},
    }

    # Generate hash of entry (for tamper detection)
    entry_json = json.dumps(entry_data, sort_keys=True)
    entry_hash = hashlib.sha256(entry_json.encode()).hexdigest()[:16]

    # Format log line
    log_line = f"\n[{timestamp}] [CLI] {action} (exit_code: {exit_code}, duration: {duration_sec:.1f}s, hash: {entry_hash})"
    if details:
        for key, value in details.items():
            log_line += f"\n  ‚Üí {key}: {value}"

    # Append to ERGON
    ergon_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    with ergon_path.open("a") as f:
        f.write(log_line + "\n")
```

---

## One-Block Installation

To get started immediately:

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

PROJECT_DIR="/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE"
CODE_BIN="$PROJECT_DIR/CODE/bin"

# 1. Back up current omega
cp "$CODE_BIN/omega" "$CODE_BIN/omega.backup.$(date +%s)"

```python
# In omega_cli/commands.py

def get_smart_suggestions(user_input: str, history: list) -> list:
    """
    Return 3‚Äď5 contextual command suggestions based on:
    1. Recent command history
    2. Skill manifest matches
    3. Current git state
    4. Error recovery patterns
    """
    suggestions = []

    # Load skill manifest
    manifest_path = Path.home() / "OMEGA_WORKSPACE" / "claude-skill-bundle" / "skills" / "skills-manifest.json"
    try:
        manifest = json.loads(manifest_path.read_text())
        skills = manifest.get("skills", {})
    except:
        skills = {}

    # Fuzzy match input against skill names
    from difflib import get_close_matches
    matches = get_close_matches(user_input.lower(), list(skills.keys()), n=2, cutoff=0.6)
    for match in matches:
        suggestions.append({
            "type": "skill",
            "label": f"/skill {match}",
            "description": skills[match].get("description", ""),
        })

    # Suggest based on recent error patterns
    if history and "error" in history[-1].get("content", "").lower():
        error_msg = history[-1]["content"]
        if "npm" in error_msg or "build" in error_msg:
            suggestions.append({
                "type": "skill",
                "label": "/skill npm-smoke-test-fixer",
                "description": "Diagnose and fix npm build failures",
            })
        if "db" in error_msg or "database" in error_msg:
            suggestions.append({
                "type": "monitor",
                "label": "/monitor",
                "description": "Check database status",
            })

    return suggestions[:5]  # Return top 5

# In main prompt loop:
suggestions = get_smart_suggestions(user_input, history)
if suggestions:
    console.print("[dim]Suggestions:[/dim]")
    for i, s in enumerate(suggestions, 1):
        console.print(f"  {i}. [{s['type']}] {s['label']} ‚ÄĒ {s['description']}")
```

### Snippet 2: Real-Time DB Monitoring
```python
# In omega_cli/monitoring.py

import psycopg2
from rich.live import Live
from rich.table import Table

def monitor_databases(interval: int = 10) -> None:
    """
    Real-time dashboard showing DB1/DB2 utilization, API health, session metrics.
    """
    db1_url = os.environ.get("OMEGA_DB_URL")
    db2_url = os.environ.get("OMEGA_DB2_URL")

    def get_table():
        table = Table(title="OmegA Status Dashboard")
        table.add_column("Component", style="cyan")
        table.add_column("Status", style="magenta")
        table.add_column("Metrics", style="green")

        # DB1 status
        try:
            conn1 = psycopg2.connect(db1_url)
            cur1 = conn1.cursor()
            cur1.execute("SELECT pg_database_size(current_database()) / (512.0 * 1024 * 1024) * 100")
            db1_util = cur1.fetchone()[0]
            conn1.close()
            status1 = "ūüüĘ OK" if db1_util < 90 else "ūüĒī CRITICAL"
            table.add_row("DB1 (personal)", status1, f"{db1_util:.1f}%")
        except:
            table.add_row("DB1 (personal)", "‚ö†ÔłŹ ERROR", "Connection failed")

        # DB2 status
        try:
            conn2 = psycopg2.connect(db2_url)
            cur2 = conn2.cursor()
            cur2.execute("SELECT pg_database_size(current_database()) / (512.0 * 1024 * 1024) * 100")
            db2_util = cur2.fetchone()[0]
            conn2.close()
            status2 = "ūüüĘ OK" if db2_util < 85 else ("ūüü° HIGH" if db2_util < 90 else "ūüĒī CRITICAL")
            table.add_row("DB2 (technical)", status2, f"{db2_util:.1f}%")
        except:
            table.add_row("DB2 (technical)", "‚ö†ÔłŹ ERROR", "Connection failed")

        # API health
        try:
            resp = httpx.get(f"{API_BASE}/health", timeout=5)
            api_status = "ūüüĘ OK" if resp.status_code == 200 else "ūüü° DEGRADED"
            latency = f"{resp.elapsed.total_seconds() * 1000:.0f}ms"
        except:
            api_status = "ūüĒī DOWN"
            latency = "‚ÄĒ"
        table.add_row("API Gateway", api_status, f"latency: {latency}")

        return table

    with Live(get_table(), refresh_per_second=0.2, console=console) as live:
        try:
            while True:
                time.sleep(interval)
                live.update(get_table())
        except KeyboardInterrupt:
            console.print("\n[green]‚úď Monitoring stopped[/green]")
```

### Snippet 3: Skill Invocation
```python
# In omega_cli/skills.py

async def invoke_skill(skill_name: str, args: list) -> str:
    """
    Route a skill invocation to the local Agent OS.
    Returns the skill's output.
    """
    # Validate skill exists in manifest
    manifest_path = Path.home() / "OMEGA_WORKSPACE" / "claude-skill-bundle" / "skills" / "skills-manifest.json"
    manifest = json.loads(manifest_path.read_text())
    if skill_name not in manifest.get("skills", {}):
        return f"ERROR: Skill '{skill_name}' not found in manifest"

    # Call Agent OS via /skill route
    payload = {
        "skill": skill_name,
        "args": args,
        "session_id": current_session_id,
    }

    try:
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=300) as client:
            async with client.stream(
                "POST", f"{API_BASE}/skills/{skill_name}",
                json=payload,
            ) as resp:
                output = ""
                async for chunk in resp.aiter_text():
                    output += chunk
                    console.print(chunk, end="")
                return output
    except Exception as e:
        return f"ERROR: {str(e)}"
```

### Snippet 4: ERGON.md Audit Logging
```python
# In omega_cli/audit.py

import hashlib

def log_to_ergon(action: str, exit_code: int, duration_sec: float, details: dict = None) -> None:
    """
    Append an audit entry to ~/NEXUS/ERGON.md with checksum for integrity.
    """
    ergon_path = Path.home() / "NEXUS" / "ERGON.md"

    # Build log entry
    timestamp = datetime.now().isoformat()
    entry_data = {
        "timestamp": timestamp,
        "cli": True,
        "action": action,
        "user": os.getenv("USER", "unknown"),
        "exit_code": exit_code,
        "duration_sec": duration_sec,
        "details": details or {},
    }

    # Generate hash of entry (for tamper detection)
    entry_json = json.dumps(entry_data, sort_keys=True)
    entry_hash = hashlib.sha256(entry_json.encode()).hexdigest()[:16]

    # Format log line
    log_line = f"\n[{timestamp}] [CLI] {action} (exit_code: {exit_code}, duration: {duration_sec:.1f}s, hash: {entry_hash})"
    if details:
        for key, value in details.items():
            log_line += f"\n  ‚Üí {key}: {value}"

    # Append to ERGON
    ergon_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    with ergon_path.open("a") as f:
        f.write(log_line + "\n")
```

"""
OmegA CLI Monitoring ‚ÄĒ Real-time DB status, API health, session metrics.
"""

import os
import time
from typing import Optional, Dict, Tuple
import psycopg2
import httpx
from datetime import datetime
from rich.table import Table
from rich.live import Live
from rich.console import Console

console = Console()


def get_db_utilization(db_url: str) -> Optional[float]:
    """Get database utilization percentage (0-100)."""
    if not db_url:
        return None

    try:
        conn = psycopg2.connect(db_url)
        cur = conn.cursor()
        cur.execute(
            "SELECT pg_database_size(current_database()) / (512.0 * 1024 * 1024) * 100"
        )
        util = cur.fetchone()[0]
        cur.close()
        conn.close()
        return round(util, 1)
    except Exception:
        return None


def get_db_entry_count(db_url: str) -> Optional[int]:
    """Get count of entries in omega_memory_entries table."""
    if not db_url:
        return None

    try:
        conn = psycopg2.connect(db_url)
        cur = conn.cursor()
        cur.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM public.omega_memory_entries")
        count = cur.fetchone()[0]
        cur.close()
        conn.close()
        return count
    except Exception:
        return None


def check_api_health(api_base: str) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
    """Check API gateway health. Returns (is_healthy, status_msg)."""
    try:
        resp = httpx.get(f"{api_base}/health", timeout=5)
        if resp.status_code == 200:
            latency = f"{resp.elapsed.total_seconds() * 1000:.0f}ms"
            return True, f"‚úď OK ({latency})"
        else:
            return False, f"‚úó {resp.status_code}"
    except httpx.ConnectError:
        return False, "‚úó Unreachable"
    except httpx.TimeoutException:
        return False, "‚úó Timeout"
    except Exception as e:
        return False, f"‚úó {type(e).__name__}"


def build_status_table(
    db1_url: str,
    db2_url: str,
    api_base: str,
    session_tokens: int = 0,
    session_cost: float = 0.0,
) -> Table:
    """Build the status dashboard table."""
    table = Table(title="ūüĆä OmegA Status Dashboard", show_header=True, header_style="bold cyan")
    table.add_column("Component", style="cyan", width=20)
    table.add_column("Status", style="magenta", width=15)
    table.add_column("Metrics", style="green")

    # DB1 (personal)
    db1_util = get_db_utilization(db1_url)
    if db1_util is not None:
        if db1_util > 90:
            status1 = "ūüĒī CRITICAL"
        elif db1_util > 85:
            status1 = "ūüü° HIGH"
        else:
            status1 = "ūüüĘ OK"
        table.add_row("DB1 (personal)", status1, f"{db1_util:.1f}% of 512 MB")
    else:
        table.add_row("DB1 (personal)", "‚ö†ÔłŹ ERROR", "Connection failed")

    # DB2 (technical)
    db2_util = get_db_utilization(db2_url)
    if db2_util is not None:
        if db2_util > 90:
            status2 = "ūüĒī CRITICAL"
        elif db2_util > 85:
            status2 = "ūüü° HIGH"
        else:
            status2 = "ūüüĘ OK"
        entries = get_db_entry_count(db2_url)
        entries_str = f"{entries:,} entries" if entries else "‚ÄĒ"
        table.add_row("DB2 (technical)", status2, f"{db2_util:.1f}% of 512 MB | {entries_str}")
    else:
        table.add_row("DB2 (technical)", "‚ö†ÔłŹ ERROR", "Connection failed")

    # API Gateway
    api_healthy, api_msg = check_api_health(api_base)
    api_status = "ūüüĘ OK" if api_healthy else "ūüĒī DOWN"
    table.add_row("API Gateway", api_status, api_msg)

    # Session metrics
    cost_str = f"${session_cost:.3f}" if session_cost > 0 else "‚ÄĒ"
    table.add_row(
        "Session",
        "ūüďä Active",
        f"{session_tokens:,} tokens | cost: {cost_str}",
    )

    return table


def monitor_live(
    db1_url: str,
    db2_url: str,
    api_base: str,
    interval: int = 10,
    max_duration_sec: Optional[int] = None,
) -> None:
    """
    Live-update dashboard showing DB utilization, API health, and session metrics.

    Args:
        db1_url: Database 1 connection string
        db2_url: Database 2 connection string
        api_base: API gateway base URL
        interval: Refresh interval in seconds
        max_duration_sec: Maximum monitoring duration (None = until Ctrl+C)
    """
    start_time = time.time()

    try:
        with Live(
            build_status_table(db1_url, db2_url, api_base),
            refresh_per_second=0.2,
            console=console,
        ) as live:
            while True:
                if max_duration_sec and (time.time() - start_time) > max_duration_sec:
                    console.print("\n[green]‚úď Monitoring duration reached[/green]")
                    break

                time.sleep(interval)
                table = build_status_table(db1_url, db2_url, api_base)
                live.update(table)

    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        console.print("\n[yellow]‚úď Monitoring stopped by user[/yellow]")

"""
OmegA CLI Audit ‚ÄĒ Log all CLI actions to ERGON.md with checksums for integrity.
"""

import hashlib
import json
import os
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Dict, Any, Optional


def generate_hash(data: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    """Generate SHA256 hash of audit entry (first 12 chars)."""
    entry_json = json.dumps(data, sort_keys=True)
    return hashlib.sha256(entry_json.encode()).hexdigest()[:12]


def log_cli_action(
    action: str,
    exit_code: int,
    duration_sec: float,
    details: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
) -> None:
    """
    Append a CLI action to ERGON.md with checksum for integrity.

    Args:
        action: What was executed (e.g., "resume_sweep2", "skill npm-smoke-test-fixer")
        exit_code: Exit code (0 = success)
        duration_sec: How long the action took
        details: Additional metadata (items processed, DB util, etc.)
    """
    ergon_path = Path.home() / "NEXUS" / "ERGON.md"
    ergon_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    timestamp = datetime.now().isoformat()
    entry_data = {
        "timestamp": timestamp,
        "cli": True,
        "action": action,
        "user": os.getenv("USER", "unknown"),
        "exit_code": exit_code,
        "duration_sec": round(duration_sec, 2),
        "details": details or {},
    }

    entry_hash = generate_hash(entry_data)

    # Format log entry
    log_line = f"\n[{timestamp}] [CLI] {action} (exit_code: {exit_code}, duration: {duration_sec:.1f}s, hash: {entry_hash})"

    if details:
        for key, value in details.items():
            if isinstance(value, (int, float)):
                log_line += f"\n  ‚Üí {key}: {value}"
            elif isinstance(value, str):
                log_line += f"\n  ‚Üí {key}: {value}"
            elif isinstance(value, list):
                log_line += f"\n  ‚Üí {key}: {', '.join(map(str, value))}"
            else:
                log_line += f"\n  ‚Üí {key}: {json.dumps(value)}"

    # Append to ERGON.md
    try:
        with ergon_path.open("a") as f:
            f.write(log_line + "\n")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"[WARN] Failed to log to ERGON.md: {e}")


def log_ingest_completion(
    items_processed: int,
    final_db_util: float,
    peak_db_util: float,
    success: bool,
) -> None:
    """Log completion of INBOX ingest to ERGON.md."""
    details = {
        "items_processed": items_processed,
        "final_db_util_pct": round(final_db_util, 1),
        "peak_db_util_pct": round(peak_db_util, 1),
        "status": "COMPLETED" if success else "FAILED",
    }

    action = "INBOX_INGEST_RESUMED"
    log_cli_action(
        action,
        exit_code=0 if success else 1,
        duration_sec=0,  # Will be updated by caller
        details=details,
    )


def verify_ergon_integrity() -> bool:
    """Verify ERGON.md integrity by checking hashes."""
    ergon_path = Path.home() / "NEXUS" / "ERGON.md"

    if not ergon_path.exists():
        return True  # File doesn't exist yet, no integrity check needed

    try:
        content = ergon_path.read_text()
        # TODO: Parse lines with hashes and verify integrity
        # For now, just verify file is readable and contains ERGON.md header
        return "ERGON.md" in content or len(content) == 0
    except Exception:
        return False

    entry_hash = generate_hash(entry_data)

    # Format log entry
    log_line = f"\n[{timestamp}] [CLI] {action} (exit_code: {exit_code}, duration: {duration_sec:.1f}s, hash: {entry_hash})"

    if details:
        for key, value in details.items():
            if isinstance(value, (int, float)):
                log_line += f"\n  ‚Üí {key}: {value}"
            elif isinstance(value, str):
                log_line += f"\n  ‚Üí {key}: {value}"
            elif isinstance(value, list):
                log_line += f"\n  ‚Üí {key}: {', '.join(map(str, value))}"
            else:
                log_line += f"\n  ‚Üí {key}: {json.dumps(value)}"

    # Append to ERGON.md
    try:
        with ergon_path.open("a") as f:
            f.write(log_line + "\n")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"[WARN] Failed to log to ERGON.md: {e}")


def log_ingest_completion(
    items_processed: int,
    final_db_util: float,
    peak_db_util: float,
    success: bool,
) -> None:
    """Log completion of INBOX ingest to ERGON.md."""
    details = {
        "items_processed": items_processed,
        "final_db_util_pct": round(final_db_util, 1),
        "peak_db_util_pct": round(peak_db_util, 1),
        "status": "COMPLETED" if success else "FAILED",
    }

    action = "INBOX_INGEST_RESUMED"
    log_cli_action(
        action,
        exit_code=0 if success else 1,
        duration_sec=0,  # Will be updated by caller
        details=details,
    )


def verify_ergon_integrity() -> bool:
    """Verify ERGON.md integrity by checking hashes."""
    ergon_path = Path.home() / "NEXUS" / "ERGON.md"

    if not ergon_path.exists():
        return True  # File doesn't exist yet, no integrity check needed

    try:
        content = ergon_path.read_text()
        # TODO: Parse lines with hashes and verify integrity
        # For now, just verify file is readable and contains ERGON.md header
        return "ERGON.md" in content or len(content) == 0
    except Exception:
        return False

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
OmegA ‚ÄĒ Sovereign Intelligence CLI
R ¬∑ W ¬∑ Ōú ¬∑ Y
"""

import asyncio
import json
import os
import re
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional

import httpx
from prompt_toolkit import PromptSession
from prompt_toolkit.auto_suggest import AutoSuggestFromHistory
from prompt_toolkit.formatted_text import HTML
from prompt_toolkit.history import FileHistory
from prompt_toolkit.key_binding import KeyBindings
from prompt_toolkit.styles import Style
from rich import box
from rich.columns import Columns
from rich.console import Console
from rich.live import Live
from rich.markdown import Markdown
from rich.panel import Panel
from rich.rule import Rule
from rich.syntax import Syntax
from rich.table import Table
from rich.text import Text
from rich.theme import Theme
from rich.tree import Tree

# ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Config ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

ENV_FILES = [
    Path.home() / ".omega" / "one-true.env",
    Path.home() / ".omega" / "gateway.env",
]
READLINE_HISTORY = Path.home() / ".omega" / "cli" / "readline_history"
SESSION_LOG      = Path.home() / ".omega" / "cli" / "history.jsonl"
SESSIONS_DIR     = Path.home() / ".omega" / "sessions"
API_BASE         = os.environ.get("OMEGA_API_URL", "https://omega-sovereign.vercel.app")

for _p in [READLINE_HISTORY.parent, SESSIONS_DIR]:
    _p.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

# ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Risk classification ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

READONLY_CMDS   = {"ls","ll","la","cat","head","tail","echo","pwd","env","printenv",
                   "which","file","find","grep","rg","fd","wc","diff","stat","tree",
                   "git status","git log","git diff","git show","git branch",
                   "ps","top","df","du","free","uname","whoami","id","date","uptime"}
HIGHRISK_PATTERNS = re.compile(
    r"\b(rm|rmdir|mv|cp|chmod|chown|sudo|dd|mkfs|fdisk|parted|"
    r"git\s+push|git\s+reset\s+--hard|git\s+rebase|git\s+force|"
    r"pip\s+install|npm\s+install|apt\s+install|apt-get)\b",
    re.IGNORECASE
)

def classify_risk(cmd: str) -> str:
    base = cmd.strip().split()[0] if cmd.strip() else ""
    if any(cmd.strip().startswith(r) for r in READONLY_CMDS):
        return "readonly"
    if HIGHRISK_PATTERNS.search(cmd):
        return "high"
    return "standard"

# ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Theme ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

THEME = Theme({
    "omega":   "bold #a855f7",
    "user":    "bold #22d4f5",
    "dim":     "dim #4a4947",
    "gold":    "bold #e8af34",
    "green":   "#6bcf7f",
    "red":     "bold #ff6b6b",
    "yellow":  "#ffd93d",
    "muted":   "#797876",
    "border":  "#2a2826",
    "banner":  "bold #22d4f5",
    "high":    "bold #ff6b6b",
    "std":     "#ffd93d",
    "low":     "#6bcf7f",
    "code":    "#22d4f5",
})

console = Console(theme=THEME, highlight=False)

PROMPT_STYLE = Style.from_dict({
    "prompt":       "#22d4f5 bold",
    "prompt.arrow": "#a855f7 bold",
    "":             "#cdccca",
})

# ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Env ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

def load_env():
    for path in ENV_FILES:
        if not path.exists():
            continue
        for line in path.read_text().splitlines():
            line = line.strip()
            if not line or line.startswith("#") or "=" not in line:
                continue
            k, v = line.split("=", 1)
            os.environ.setdefault(k.strip(), v.strip().strip('"').strip("'"))

# ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Session ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

def new_session_id() -> str:
    return f"ő©-{datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d-%H%M')}"

def session_path(sid: str) -> Path:
    return SESSIONS_DIR / f"{sid}.json"

def save_session(sid: str, history: list, state: dict):
    data = {
        "session_id": sid,
        "created_at": state.get("created_at", datetime.now().isoformat()),
        "updated_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
        "messages": history,
        "state": state,
    }
    path = session_path(sid)
    path.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2))

def load_session(sid: str) -> Optional[dict]:
    path = session_path(sid)
    if not path.exists():
        return None
    return json.loads(path.read_text())

def list_sessions() -> list:
    sessions = []
    for p in sorted(SESSIONS_DIR.glob("*.json"), reverse=True)[:20]:
        try:
            d = json.loads(p.read_text())
            sessions.append(d)
        except Exception:
            pass
    return sessions

# ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Git context ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ

def git_info() -> str:
    try:
        branch = subprocess.check_output(
            ["git", "rev-parse", "--abbrev-ref", "HEAD"],
            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL, text=True
        ).strip()
        dirty = subprocess.call(
            ["git", "diff", "--quiet"],
            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL
        ) != 0
        return f"{branch}{'*' if dirty else ''}"
    except Exception:
        return ""
